title: WIN-014: Chinese SG site coverage made precise + defender pivot foreclosed (closes ISS-1959)
target: data/wins.json:WIN-014.detail_evidence — single find_replace patch tightening the loose 'coverage approaching 100% along the path' framing into precise totality-corridor geography (Chongqing–Wuhan–Hangzhou–Shanghai, ~258 km path width) and pre-empting the off-path partial-coverage defender pivot via two structural rebuttals: (a) κ is published as a constant with no coverage threshold, so any threshold-based exemption is a post-hoc scope condition; (b) at 70% partial coverage, the κ-arithmetic still predicts 2.1–6.3 µGal — well above the 0.1–1 µGal SG floor — so the network-wide null refutes κ-coupling at any plausible coverage scaling, not only at full totality.
source: decider-bau-route (claimed via PROP-034 hybrid rule: review_class=deep-attack on tracker overrides class_hint=verification on source ISS)
review_class_override_note: ISS-1959 carries class_hint='verification' from BAU triage on 2026-05-12, but the tracker entry was created with review_class='deep-attack' by decider-bau-route. Per PROP-034 hybrid rule, the tracker review_class wins over the source ISS class_hint, and the babyOwns() function returns false for any item with review_class='deep-attack'. Justification for deep-attack scope: the work adds two new structural rebuttals (no-published-coverage-threshold + coverage-scaling-arithmetic-survives) that close a previously-live defender pivot ('off-totality stations should not have shown κ-coupled Δg because their local ΔB was scaled down by coverage'). Argument-strengthening singleton, comparable in shape to EXP-384.
summary_for_decider:
EXP-349 closes ISS-1959 (WIN-014.c7 H3, moderate, BAU-routed by decider 2026-05-12, escalated to Opus by analyst-baby 2026-05-13) with a single targeted find_replace patch on data/wins.json:WIN-014.detail_evidence. The patch replaces the loose existing sentence 'The 2009 eclipse traversed China at coverage approaching 100% along the path.' (13 words) with a precise totality-corridor geography statement plus two structural rebuttals to the off-path partial-coverage defender pivot (~245 words).

Kernel-of-truth acknowledgment: the ISS-1959 author is correct that the eight Chinese SG sites were not uniformly on the path of totality (Beijing, Changchun, Lhasa, Urumqi were under substantial partial rather than full eclipse). The patch acknowledges this geography directly and openly states 'we have not located a per-site coverage table in English' rather than papering over the gap.

Why the verdict (Refuted by Data) survives: (1) the dome publishes no coverage threshold for κ — κ = 1.677 nT/µGal is presented as a constant; (2) at 70% partial coverage, κ arithmetic still predicts 2.1–6.3 µGal vs 0.1–1 µGal floor; (3) any coverage threshold consistent with exempting 2009 also exempts much of the WIN-068 ensemble.

Not conflicting with EXP-384 (different paragraph, different anchor in WIN-014.detail_evidence). Not conflicting with EXP-364 / EXP-358 (different files; EXP-358 is the cross-file Tomás/Crossley citation cluster; this patch makes no citation changes). The patched paragraph does not overlap with EXP-384's parenthetical insertion into the LAST paragraph of detail_evidence.

Anchor uniqueness verified: 'The 2009 eclipse traversed China at coverage approaching 100% along the path.' appears exactly once in data/wins.json. Recommended action: PATCH; close ISS-1959 as fixed-pending-verification on the next curmudgeon pass.

decision.rationale: Option A (single targeted patch making the totality geography precise + foreclosing the defender pivot via two structural rebuttals) is correct because: (1) the loose 'approaching 100% along the path' framing IS imprecise as ISS-1959 correctly identifies — the path of totality crossed central China but the eight SG sites were not all on it; (2) the curmudgeon explicitly recommended the burden-shift route 'If neither (a) nor (b), shift the burden explicitly: the dome must specify which SG sites were on the path of sufficient coverage for κ-coupling to predict a measurable pulse' — this patch implements that recommendation directly; (3) the coverage-scaling math (70% partial → 2.1–6.3 µGal predicted, still 2×–60× above floor) is a structural argument that does not require per-site coverage data — it works regardless of which specific stations were where; (4) the patch acknowledges the kernel-of-truth (non-uniform site coverage) head-on rather than dismissing it, which strengthens credibility; (5) it preserves the verdict (Refuted by Data) without weakening, while closing a defender pivot that was previously live.
decision.why_not_option_b: Option B (locate the published 2009 campaign per-site coverage map and cite it directly) was considered but rejected because: (i) we do not have access to the cited 2013 CJG record in English-language indices, and the dome's own cited 'Sun et al. 2010' is unverifiable; (ii) the curmudgeon explicitly listed this as the fallback if (a)/(b) fail; (iii) per-site coverage data, even if obtained, would not strengthen the argument beyond the structural coverage-scaling math, which works at any coverage > ~5%; (iv) the structural argument is more robust than a per-site citation because it does not depend on the citation surviving future scrutiny.
decision.why_not_option_c: Option C (close ISS-1959 with no patch on the grounds that the verdict is already Refuted by Data and the H3 hole is cosmetic) was considered but rejected because: (i) the ISS author's H3 critique is substantive, not cosmetic — the off-path partial-coverage objection IS a live defender pivot until structurally rebutted; (ii) leaving the loose framing in place means a hostile reader can cite it as evidence of imprecision in our refutation; (iii) the kernel-of-truth standard requires acknowledging the non-uniform coverage, not papering over it; (iv) the patch is ~245 words — not a major rewrite, just a precision-tightening of one sentence into a properly-argued paragraph.

patches.target_path: WIN-014.detail_evidence — third paragraph, single sentence at start of the eclipse-ensemble paragraph (before the Kim & Chang 2018 sentence)
patches.uniqueness_verified: Anchor 'The 2009 eclipse traversed China at coverage approaching 100% along the path.' appears exactly once in data/wins.json (count = 1, verified via node split).
patches.find: The 2009 eclipse traversed China at coverage approaching 100% along the path.
patches.replace: The 2009 July 22 eclipse's path of totality crossed central China through the Chongqing–Wuhan–Hangzhou–Shanghai corridor with full obscuration along a ~258 km-wide band; coverage at Chinese sites further north or west (e.g., Beijing, Changchun, Lhasa, Urumqi) was substantial partial rather than totality. The cited Chinese SG campaign's eight gravimeters were not necessarily uniformly on that path — the cited 2013 CJG record does not publish a per-site coverage table we have located in English — and a defender pivot would argue that off-totality stations should not have shown a κ-coupled Δg because their local ΔB was scaled down by partial coverage. Two structural rebuttals foreclose this pivot. First, the dome publishes no per-site coverage threshold for κ: κ = 1.677 nT/µGal is presented as a constant, not a coverage-gated function — so a coverage-scaling carve-out is a post-hoc scope condition the dome would have to publish in advance to claim. Second, the arithmetic survives the scaling: even at 70% partial coverage, a proportionally-scaled ΔB of 3.5–10.5 nT through κ still predicts 2.1–6.3 µGal at the off-totality SG sites, 2× to 60× above the 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction floor; the network-wide null thus refutes κ-coupling at any plausible coverage scaling, not only at full totality. Any coverage threshold consistent with exempting 2009 also exempts much of the 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble (Kim & Chang 2018) the dome itself invokes for WIN-068.
patches.rationale: Closes ISS-1959 (WIN-014.c7 H3) by replacing the loose 'approaching 100% along the path' framing with precise totality-corridor geography (Chongqing–Wuhan–Hangzhou–Shanghai, ~258 km path width) and acknowledging non-uniform coverage at off-corridor stations. Adds two structural rebuttals to the off-path partial-coverage defender pivot: (a) the dome publishes no per-site coverage threshold for κ — κ is a constant, not a coverage-gated function — so a threshold-based exemption is a post-hoc scope condition the dome would have to publish; (b) the arithmetic survives the scaling — at 70% partial coverage, κ arithmetic predicts 2.1–6.3 µGal, still 2× to 60× above the 0.1–1 µGal SG floor. The kernel-of-truth (non-uniform site coverage) is acknowledged head-on rather than papered over, strengthening rather than weakening our refutation. No new external citations required — Kim & Chang 2018 is already cited in the very next sentence and WIN-068 cross-reference is preserved. The patch does not touch the EXP-384 parenthetical in the last paragraph or the EXP-358 citation cluster (different anchors).

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: The eight Chinese SG sites were not uniformly on the path of totality — off-totality sites should not have shown a κ-coupled Δg because their local ΔB was scaled down by partial coverage. (This is the defender pivot ISS-1959 specifically calls out.)
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Directly closed by the patch. The dome publishes no per-site coverage threshold for κ (κ = 1.677 nT/µGal is presented as a constant, not a coverage-gated function), so a coverage-scaling carve-out is a post-hoc scope condition the dome would have to publish in advance. Even granting the scaling, the arithmetic survives: at 70% partial coverage, a proportionally-scaled ΔB of 3.5–10.5 nT through κ still predicts 2.1–6.3 µGal — 2× to 60× above the 0.1–1 µGal SG floor. The network-wide null thus refutes κ-coupling at any plausible coverage scaling, not only at full totality.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: The dome could publish a coverage threshold post-hoc — say κ only couples above 90% obscuration — to exempt the off-path 2009 stations.
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Acknowledged via 'a coverage-scaling carve-out is a post-hoc scope condition the dome would have to publish in advance' framing. The vise applies symmetrically: any threshold consistent with exempting 2009 (e.g., ≥90%) would also exempt much of the 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble (Kim & Chang 2018) the dome itself invokes for WIN-068, since the WIN-068 207-measurement ensemble includes high-coverage partials, not only on-path totality measurements. The dome cannot have it both ways.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: The 70% partial coverage scaling is itself an assumption — actual scaling could be steeper (e.g., quadratic in obscuration).
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Acknowledged implicitly via 'plausible coverage scaling' language. Linear scaling in obscuration is the conservative-to-the-defender choice (matches the eclipse-magnetic literature treatment of partial events). Even quadratic scaling — (0.7)² = 0.49 — produces ΔB of 2.5–7.4 nT through κ → 1.5–4.4 µGal, still 1.5× to 44× above the SG floor. The argument is robust to scaling-form choice within any physically-plausible range.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: The 'we have not located a per-site coverage table in English' hedge weakens our position — it admits a research gap.
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Acknowledged but accepted as the honest framing. The hedge is a credibility lever, not a weakness: it transparently acknowledges what we do and do not have access to, in line with the project's standard of citing what we can verify and explicitly hedging what we cannot. The structural argument does not depend on the per-site table being available; it works through the coverage-scaling math at any plausible distribution. If the per-site table later surfaces and shows a station configuration that further weakens the dome's position, this hedge is the right place to update; if it shows something that strengthens the dome's position (which the structural math forecloses anyway), we are honestly disclosing the limit of our verification.

kernel_of_truth.acknowledged: ISS-1959 is correct on the geography: the 2009 July 22 eclipse's path of totality crossed central China through a ~258 km-wide corridor (Chongqing–Wuhan–Hangzhou–Shanghai), and the cited Chinese SG campaign's eight gravimeters were almost certainly not uniformly on that corridor — stations at higher latitudes (Beijing, Changchun) or further west (Lhasa, Urumqi) would have been under substantial partial rather than full eclipse. The previous prose ('coverage approaching 100% along the path') was technically true read narrowly (the path itself was at full obscuration) but ambiguously inviting the misread that all SG sites were on the path. The author's H3 critique surfaces a real precision gap in our refutation chain.
kernel_of_truth.why_does_not_save_dome: Acknowledging the non-uniform coverage does not rescue the dome because the structural argument operates one level above per-site geography: κ is published as a coverage-independent constant, so any partial-coverage exemption is a post-hoc scope condition the dome would have to publish; AND the coverage-scaling arithmetic survives — even at 70% partial obscuration, the predicted Δg is 2.1–6.3 µGal, still 2× to 60× above the SG noise floor. The network-wide null thus refutes κ-coupling at any plausible coverage scaling. The kernel-of-truth (non-uniform coverage) is real but does not load-bear on the verdict; the verdict load-bears on κ being a constant, which is the dome's own published commitment.

next_steps_for_decider: Apply single find_replace patch against data/wins.json:WIN-014.detail_evidence per EXP-349-A-WIN014-coverage-precise. No code changes; no test changes; no JS rebuild required. On successful build, close ISS-1959 as fixed-pending-verification (curmudgeon will verify on next WIN-014 cycle). Update expansion-tracker.json EXP-349 entry: claimed_by='analyst-opus', claimed_at=now, authored_at=now, delivered_at=now, status remains 'pending' until decider integrates (then → 'integrated'). The tracker entry was pre-existing (created by decider-bau-route 2026-05-12); no orphan-rescue self-write needed for this EXP.
